description_long: This object is a vibrant red flower with two prominent blossoms, each displaying broad, overlapping petals with a subtle gradient from deep red to pinkish hues. The petals are slightly curved and have a soft, velvety appearance, with visible stamen protruding from the center of each bloom. The flower is supported by a slender, brownish-green stem that branches to hold both blossoms at different heights. Several green, ovate leaves emerge from the stem, adding contrast to the vivid flowers. The overall structure is delicate and naturalistic, resembling a hibiscus or similar ornamental flower often found in urban parks or gardens. The arrangement and coloration make it visually striking and suitable for decorative planting in public or residential urban spaces.
description: A striking red flower with two large, overlapping blossoms and green ovate leaves on a slender stem. The petals are broad and velvety, with visible stamen and a natural, ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: Two blossoms are visible, one facing forward and the other angled to the side, showing the flower's layered petal structure and central stamen.
description_view_1: Both blossoms are seen from the side, highlighting the branching stem and the spread of the green leaves.
description_view_2: The front blossom is fully visible, with the second blossom angled away, emphasizing the petal curvature and color gradient.
description_view_3: A single blossom is seen head-on, displaying the symmetrical arrangement of petals and the central stamen, with leaves extending to either side.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with overlapping layers, creating a symmetrical and full appearance typical of many ornamental flowers.
